supplementEntry.name: Magnesium L-Threonate
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Six RCTs (five positive, one negative) with large effect size (d=0.91) in primary cognitive trial. Sample sizes moderate (44-109 per trial). Most studies industry-sponsored. No large-scale independent replication yet.
mechanismsOfAction: Elevation of brain magnesium concentration via BBB-permeable threonate transport
Increased hippocampal synaptic density and synaptogenesis
Enhancement of NMDA receptor signaling and synaptic plasticity
Upregulation of NR2B-containing NMDA receptors in prefrontal cortex
Enhanced short-term and long-term synaptic potentiation
TNF-alpha inhibition in neuropathic pain pathways

drugInteractions: May reduce absorption of tetracycline and quinolone antibiotics
Standard magnesium interaction profile with diuretics

effectSizes.sleepQuality: Significant improvements in deep sleep, REM, light sleep duration

keyCitations.title: Efficacy and Safety of MMFS-01, a Synapse Density Enhancer, for Treating Cognitive Impairment in Older Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
Magnesium-L-threonate improves sleep quality and daytime functioning in adults with self-reported sleep problems: A randomized controlled trial
A Magtein, Magnesium L-Threonate, -Based Formula Improves Brain Cognitive Functions in Healthy Chinese Adults
Enhancement of learning and memory by elevating brain magnesium
Oral application of magnesium-L-threonate enhances analgesia and reduces the dosage of opioids needed in advanced cancer patients

mechanisms.mechanism: Elevation of brain magnesium via BBB-permeable threonate transport

evidence.title: Enhancement of learning and memory by elevating brain magnesium
evidence.findings: MgT elevated brain Mg levels unlike other Mg forms. Enhanced learning, working memory, short- and long-term memory. Increased synaptic density in hippocampus and functional presynaptic release sites.
evidence.methodology: Rat model comparing MgT to other magnesium compounds for brain bioavailability and cognitive outcomes

mechanisms.mechanism: Increased hippocampal synaptic density and synaptogenesis

evidence.title: Enhancement of learning and memory by elevating brain magnesium
evidence.findings: MgT increased synaptic density in hippocampus, enhanced both short-term and long-term synaptic potentiation, and increased functional presynaptic release sites.
evidence.methodology: Electrophysiology and synaptic density quantification in rat hippocampal neurons

mechanisms.mechanism: Enhancement of NMDA receptor signaling and NR2B upregulation

evidence.title: Enhancement of learning and memory by elevating brain magnesium
evidence.findings: Upregulation of NR2B-containing NMDA receptors in prefrontal cortex. Enhanced NMDA receptor signaling underlying synaptic plasticity improvements.
evidence.methodology: Molecular and electrophysiological analysis of NMDA receptor subunit expression

evidence.title: The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Improved HRV and reduced resting heart rate, indicating autonomic nervous system modulation alongside cognitive improvements.
evidence.methodology: 6-week RCT with objective HRV and heart rate monitoring

benefits.specificClaim: Significant improvement in overall cognitive ability in older adults with cognitive impairment

evidence.title: Efficacy and Safety of MMFS-01, a Synapse Density Enhancer, for Treating Cognitive Impairment in Older Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
evidence.findings: Overall cognitive ability improved significantly relative to placebo (p=0.003; Cohen's d=0.91). Cognitive fluctuation decreased. Treatment nearly restored impaired executive function.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled RCT in adults aged 50-70 with cognitive impairment

evidence.title: The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Significant improvements in overall cognitive performance (NIH Total Cognition Composite, p=0.043), reaction time (p=0.031), brain cognitive age reduced by 7.5 years.
evidence.methodology: 6-week double-blind RCT in healthy adults aged 18-45 with 2g/day Magtein

benefits.specificClaim: Improvement in memory quotient scores across all subcategories

evidence.title: A Magtein, Magnesium L-Threonate, -Based Formula Improves Brain Cognitive Functions in Healthy Chinese Adults
evidence.findings: 2g/day Magtein-PS for 30 days: significant improvements in all five subcategories of the Clinical Memory Test and overall memory quotient scores. Older participants showed greater improvement.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in healthy Chinese adults aged 18-65

benefits.specificClaim: Improved sleep quality including deep sleep, REM, and daytime functioning

evidence.title: Magnesium-L-threonate improves sleep quality and daytime functioning in adults with self-reported sleep problems: A randomized controlled trial
evidence.findings: 1g/day MgT for 21 days: MgT group maintained sleep quality while placebo declined. Objective improvements in deep sleep, REM sleep, and light sleep duration. Subjective improvements in energy, mood, mental alertness, and daytime productivity.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, parallel-arm RCT in adults aged 35-55 with self-reported sleep problems

evidence.title: The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: Self-reported sleep impairment improved (p=0.043), but objective sleep metrics showed no difference vs. placebo.
evidence.methodology: 6-week double-blind RCT with both subjective and objective sleep measures

benefits.specificClaim: Improved regional cerebral metabolism and cognitive functioning in dementia patients

evidence.title: Open Label Trial of Magnesium L-Threonate in Patients with Dementia
evidence.findings: Significant improvement in regional cerebral metabolism and global index of cognitive functioning after 12 weeks. Higher RBC magnesium correlated with cognitive and executive function gains.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot in dementia patients with brain imaging

safety.claim: Good safety profile with high tolerability across multiple RCTs

evidence.title: The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: 98% of participants reported good or excellent tolerability. Adverse event rate: 10% Magtein vs 8% placebo (not significantly different).
evidence.methodology: 6-week RCT with systematic adverse event monitoring

evidence.title: Magnesium-L-threonate improves sleep quality and daytime functioning in adults with self-reported sleep problems: A randomized controlled trial
evidence.findings: Well tolerated, no major side effects reported at 1g/day for 21 days.

safety.claim: Ineffective for post-surgical pain in breast cancer patients but no safety concerns

evidence.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial to Evaluate the Therapeutic Effect of Magnesium-L-Threonate Supplementation for Persistent Pain After Breast Cancer Surgery
evidence.findings: MgT did not effectively prevent persistent pain after breast cancer surgery, nor provide sufficient pain relief over placebo. No significant improvements in pain, mood, sleep quality, or cognitive function. No safety concerns raised.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in post-surgical breast cancer patients

dosage.dosageRange: 1,000-2,000 mg/day magnesium L-threonate (yielding ~72-144 mg elemental Mg)
dosage.claim: Dose range established across multiple RCTs with cognitive and sleep endpoints

evidence.title: The effects of magnesium L-threonate (Magtein) on cognitive performance and sleep quality in adults: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
evidence.findings: 2g/day Magtein for 6 weeks showed significant cognitive improvements and sleep benefits.

evidence.title: Magnesium-L-threonate improves sleep quality and daytime functioning in adults with self-reported sleep problems: A randomized controlled trial
evidence.findings: 1g/day MgT for 21 days was effective for sleep quality improvements.
evidence.methodology: Parallel-arm RCT with objective sleep metrics
